EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The rainfall deficit across the USVI improved some on August, especially for St. Croix. However, a Severe Drought continued for St. Thomas and St. Croix, and an Extreme Drought continued for St. John.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The drought event across the USVI is a concern to agricultural interests, as hot and dry days increase water demands, while stressing pastures, crops and native vegetation.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The rainfall deficit across the USVI did not improve too much in July. Severe Drought continues for St. Thomas and St. Croix, while an Extreme Drought prevails at Saint John.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The drought event across the USVI is a concern to agricultural interests, as hot and dry days increase water demands, while stressing pastures, crops and native vegetation. Additionally, the groundwater reserves kept decreasing. Some agricultural interests are buying up to 3000 gallons of water daily to keep their products relatively healthy. Ranchers are also buying other supplements for the livestock. The hey to feet of the horses is scarce. A fire ban is now in effect for all the territory.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Pulses of large, long-period northerly swells impacted the northwest to east coasts of the islands during the first and second weeks of December. This caused dangerous breaking wave action along the Atlantic coastline, life-threatening rip currents, and minor coastal flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A report from the public indicated coastal flooding along the restaurant Rum Runners parking lot near the Christiansted boardwalk.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The rainfall deficit across the USVI worsened, with Severe Drought conditions for St. Thomas, and Extreme Drought conditions for Saint John and Saint Croix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The drought event across the USVI is a concern to agricultural interests, as hot and dry days increase water demands, while stressing pastures, crops and native vegetation. Additionally, the groundwater reserves kept decreasing. A private well in St. Thomas has gone dry for the second time in forty-years. Some agricultural interests are buying up to 3000 gallons of water daily to keep their products relatively healthy. Ranchers are also buying other supplements for the livestock. The hey to feet of the horses is scarce. A fire ban is now in effect for all the territory.

CZ_NAME: ST. THOMAS / ST. JOHN AND ADJACENT ISLANDS
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The drought classification significantly improved across the U.S. Virgin Islands, mainly due to rains brought by Hurricane Fiona. Drought conditions were completely erased along St. John and St. Thomas, while abnormally dry conditions still linger over St. Croix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Above normal precipitation was received across the northern Virgin Islands during September, and the severe drought conditions were erased along these areas. In general, over 6 inches of rain fell during the month, with a few isolated areas receiving around 15 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The drought classification significantly improved across the U.S. Virgin Islands, mainly due to rains brought by Hurricane Fiona. Drought conditions were completely erased along St. John and St. Thomas, while abnormally dry conditions still linger over St. Croix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Drought conditions improved below severe criteria during September, mainly due to rains brought by Hurricane Fiona. However, the island remained under Abnormally Dry (D0) conditions for the rest of the month.
